cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The user is asking about the experiment result from the study mentioned in the article. The question is how the students performed in the experiment. First, I need to recall the details from the text. The study had 143 undergrads writing self-advice before doing puzzles. Half used first-person ("I"), the other half second-person ("You"). The results said the second-person group did better—they completed more puzzles and had a more positive attitude. Then they also looked at exercise advice: second-person group again reported being more positive and planning to exercise more. The question gives options A to D. Looking at the options: A) First-person better attitude. But the article says the second-person had better attitude, so A is wrong. B) Those who talked to themselves completed most. But the experiment was about writing, not talking. The article mentions that the researchers asked them to write, not say it out loud. So B is probably incorrect because the correct method was writing, and the correct group was second-person. C) The ones who wrote
